stories.lens: For Indian cricket followers, Tucker's appointment confirms the opposition's line-up ahead of what will be a revealing T20 series, as India's selectors look to assess their squad depth against competitive international opposition.
stories.article:
Lorcan Tucker has been confirmed as Ireland's captain for the T20 International series against India, The Hindu reported. The wicketkeeper-batter, one of Ireland's most experienced white-ball cricketers, will lead the side as they prepare to take on one of the most demanding opponents in world T20 cricket.

Tucker's appointment reflects Ireland's belief in him not just as a performer behind the stumps and with the bat, but as a leader capable of marshalling a young squad against elite opposition. His calm demeanour and familiarity with pressure situations across Ireland's recent international fixtures make him a natural choice for the captaincy.

The T20I series against India is one of the most high-profile assignments in Ireland's cricket calendar, offering them prime exposure and the chance to upset a side that, while rotating its squad at times, never lacks for depth or quality. For India, the series is a platform to try combinations and blood newer players — a context in which Ireland will be looking to take full advantage of any opportunity given.

Details of the series schedule — match dates and venues — were separately confirmed by The Indian Express, with India vs Ireland T20 Series 2026 fixtures available for fans tracking the upcoming international action.
key_facts: Lorcan Tucker named Ireland T20I captain vs India
Tucker is Ireland's wicketkeeper-batter and an experienced white-ball player
Series is Ireland's most high-profile T20 bilateral assignment

stories.headline: 9 South OTT releases to watch this week: Baahubali The Torchbearer to Arjun Sarja's Blast and Lingam
stories.what: South Indian OTT content gets a significant boost this week, with nine new releases hitting streaming platforms. The lineup includes Baahubali The Torchbearer, an extension of the iconic Baahubali franchise, alongside Arjun Sarja's Blast and Lingam, two of the more eagerly anticipated south films making their streaming debut. Pinkvilla's roundup covers the full slate, which spans Tamil, Telugu, Kannada, and Malayalam content, reflecting the sustained appetite for South Indian cinema on digital platforms.
stories.lens: The Baahubali franchise returning in any form is a major streaming event for its enormous pan-India fanbase, and its arrival alongside nine other South releases makes this a standout week for regional OTT content.
stories.article:
South Indian streaming gets a landmark moment this week with the arrival of Baahubali The Torchbearer on OTT, joining a broader slate of nine new South releases that Pinkvilla has catalogued for the week ahead. The Baahubali name carries an almost unparalleled weight in Indian pop culture, and any new entry in that universe is guaranteed to command massive viewership.

Also in the mix are Arjun Sarja's Blast and Lingam — two films featuring the veteran South Indian actor that have been building anticipation ahead of their streaming releases. Arjun Sarja remains a major draw for Kannada and Tamil audiences, and the back-to-back arrival of two of his films in the same streaming week is notable.

The nine-title South OTT release slate this week underscores how dramatically the streaming landscape has shifted for regional Indian cinema. Where once South films relied on theatrical runs to reach Hindi-belt audiences, OTT platforms now give them immediate all-India — and often global — reach.

For South cinema fans, this is one of the richer streaming weeks in recent months, offering a blend of franchise entertainment, action, and drama across multiple languages.
key_facts: Baahubali The Torchbearer among South OTT releases this week
Arjun Sarja's Blast and Lingam also streaming this week
Total of 9 South Indian OTT releases this week
Covers Tamil, Telugu, Kannada and Malayalam content

stories.headline: New OTT releases this week (June 22-28, 2026): 'Raja Shivaji', 'Gram Chikitsalay' Season 2, 'House of the Dragon' Season 3 and more
stories.what: This week's OTT slate is one of the more diverse in recent memory, spanning Indian regional content, Hindi originals, and major international titles. Highlights include Raja Shivaji, Gram Chikitsalay Season 2, and the highly anticipated House of the Dragon Season 3, all dropping between June 22 and 28, 2026. The Times of India's roundup signals a packed week for streaming subscribers across platforms, with content catering to audiences from Bollywood devotees to fantasy-drama fans.
stories.lens: With House of the Dragon Season 3 and multiple Indian originals arriving in the same week, subscribers across OTT platforms have a genuinely stacked viewing calendar to navigate.
stories.article:
The week of June 22 to 28, 2026 brings one of the busiest OTT release windows of the year, with major titles landing across multiple streaming platforms. Topping the international marquee is House of the Dragon Season 3, the Game of Thrones prequel that has commanded enormous global viewership, returning to screens this week.

On the Indian content front, Raja Shivaji and the second season of Gram Chikitsalay — the rural medical drama that won critical praise in its first outing — are among the most anticipated domestic releases. The breadth of this week's slate, catalogued by The Times of India, reflects how aggressively streaming platforms are competing for subscriber attention with simultaneous launches.

The convergence of international prestige television and homegrown Indian storytelling in a single week underlines the maturing OTT ecosystem in India, where audiences now expect a continuous flow of both local and global content. Platforms have increasingly learned to time big-ticket international drops alongside Indian originals to maximise engagement.

For viewers, the challenge this week will simply be deciding where to start — whether with the fire-and-blood drama of Westeros, the village medicine of Gram Chikitsalay, or the historical sweep of Raja Shivaji.
key_facts: House of the Dragon Season 3 drops this week
Raja Shivaji and Gram Chikitsalay Season 2 among Indian OTT releases
Releases span June 22–28, 2026
Multiple platforms hosting simultaneous launches

stories.headline: Top 7 classic Indian TV shows available on OTT
stories.what: India Today has put together a guide to seven classic Indian television shows now available on OTT platforms, making decades of beloved content newly accessible to younger viewers and nostalgic fans. The list spans some of the most culturally significant series in Indian TV history, which are now findable on streaming services rather than buried in broadcast archives. The roundup reflects a broader trend of OTT platforms mining legacy Indian content as a tool to expand subscriber bases.
stories.lens: For a generation that grew up watching these shows on Doordarshan and early satellite TV, their OTT availability is a genuine rediscovery — and for younger Indians, it is a first encounter with the stories that shaped their parents.
stories.article:
Decades of classic Indian television are now just a tap away, and India Today has assembled a guide to seven of the most iconic shows from Indian TV history that viewers can stream right now on OTT platforms. The list covers some of the most culturally resonant series ever made for Indian audiences — programmes that defined appointment viewing for entire generations.

The migration of classic Indian TV content to streaming platforms has been quietly accelerating over the past few years, as OTT services recognise that legacy content carries both nostalgia value for older subscribers and discovery value for younger ones who missed the original broadcasts. Titles that once aired on Doordarshan or early cable channels are being digitised and licensed for streaming.

The move also serves a cultural preservation function. Many of these shows represent high points of Indian television craft — writing, direction, and performance that holds up across time — and their availability on OTT ensures they are not lost to the analogue era.

For streaming platforms competing intensely for Indian subscriber attention, curating and surfacing classic content alongside new originals is an increasingly important strategy — one that costs far less to acquire than fresh productions but delivers significant viewer engagement.
key_facts: India Today lists 7 classic Indian TV shows now on OTT platforms
Includes shows originally aired on Doordarshan and early cable TV
OTT platforms using legacy content to expand subscriber bases
Classic shows accessible to younger audiences for the first time digitally

stories.headline: 'Ten-year-old Shah Rukh Khan would supply samosas to us at NSD': Pankaj Kapur
stories.what: Veteran actor Pankaj Kapur has shared a charming and little-known anecdote about Shah Rukh Khan's earliest connection to the National School of Drama (NSD) in New Delhi — recalling how a ten-year-old SRK used to supply samosas to students there. The reminiscence, shared with The Indian Express, offers a rare glimpse into Shah Rukh Khan's pre-stardom childhood, rooted in the very institution that shaped many of India's finest stage and screen actors. It underscores how deeply intertwined SRK's early life was with the world of theatre.
stories.lens: For millions of Shah Rukh Khan fans, this story adds a wonderfully human footnote to the superstar's mythology — the boy who once sold snacks to drama students would go on to become the biggest movie star in India.
stories.article:
Long before the iconic poses and global stardom, Shah Rukh Khan was a ten-year-old boy supplying samosas to drama students. That is the vivid memory shared by Pankaj Kapur, one of Hindi cinema and theatre's most respected actors, in a conversation with The Indian Express.

Kapur recalled that as a young student at the National School of Drama in New Delhi, a young Shah Rukh Khan — whose father Meer Taj Mohammed Khan ran a canteen near the campus — would regularly bring samosas to the students. The anecdote places SRK at the heart of NSD's world even as a child, years before he would formally train in theatre and eventually take Bollywood by storm.

The National School of Drama has long been considered the crucible of Indian performing arts, producing talents like Naseeruddin Shah, Om Puri, and Pankaj Kapur himself. The idea that Shah Rukh Khan grew up in its orbit, supplying food to its students, adds an almost mythological quality to his journey from those corridors to becoming one of cinema's biggest global icons.

Pankaj Kapur's recollection is more than a charming trivia moment — it is a reminder of how deeply the culture of Indian theatre runs through Bollywood's biggest names, and how the roads to stardom are often paved in far more ordinary, intimate circumstances than the film industry's polished PR machinery would suggest.
key_facts: Pankaj Kapur recalls Shah Rukh Khan supplying samosas at NSD as a child
SRK was ten years old at the time, per Kapur's account
National School of Drama is where many top Indian actors trained
Anecdote shared with The Indian Express

stories.headline: These 10 Korean dramas are capturing India's streaming attention right now
stories.what: India Today has mapped out the ten Korean dramas currently drawing the most streaming attention from Indian audiences, underlining K-drama's remarkable entrenchment in Indian popular culture. The list reflects how Korean content has moved well beyond a niche urban following to become a mainstream OTT phenomenon across age groups and geographies in India. The K-drama wave now shapes viewing habits, fashion, food trends, and even language learning among young Indians.
stories.lens: The sustained dominance of Korean dramas on Indian streaming platforms is reshaping what Indian audiences expect from storytelling — and signals a long-term shift in how global content competes with homegrown entertainment.
stories.article:
Korean dramas have firmly cemented their place in the Indian streaming mainstream, and India Today's latest roundup of the ten K-dramas currently capturing the country's attention is a vivid snapshot of just how far this cultural wave has travelled. What began as a cult following among urban youth has expanded into a broad, cross-demographic audience that spans metros and smaller cities alike.

The popularity of Korean content on Indian OTT platforms has been building steadily for several years, accelerated by the global breakout of titles like Squid Game and the availability of subtitled K-dramas on Netflix, Viki, and other services. Indian viewers have responded to Korean drama's distinctive blend of melodrama, tight plotting, high production values, and emotional intensity — qualities that resonate with audiences already attuned to Indian soap operas but hungry for something visually slicker.

The trend has real cultural spillover effects: K-pop fandom, Korean beauty products, and Korean cuisine have all seen rising interest in India, often led by the same audiences who discovered the country through its dramas. Language-learning apps report Korean among the fastest-growing languages being studied by Indian users.

For the Indian entertainment industry, the K-drama phenomenon is both a competitive benchmark and a challenge — demonstrating that Indian audiences will invest deeply in foreign content if the storytelling is compelling enough, raising the bar for domestic OTT productions.
key_facts: India Today lists 10 Korean dramas trending on Indian streaming platforms
K-drama viewership has expanded beyond urban niche to mainstream Indian audiences
Korean content available on Netflix, Viki and other Indian OTT platforms
Cultural spillover includes K-pop, Korean food and language learning trends
